concepts.title: India's Mineral Reserves
concepts.description: India has significant reserves of coal, iron ore, bauxite, and manganese. Coal reserves are found mainly in Jharkhand, Odisha, and Chhattisgarh, while iron ore reserves are found in Odisha, Jharkhand, and Karnataka.

concepts.title: Classification of Minerals
concepts.description: Minerals can be classified into two main categories: metallic and non-metallic minerals. Metallic minerals are further divided into ferrous and non-ferrous minerals.

concepts.title: Types of Energy Resources
concepts.description: Energy resources can be broadly classified into two types: conventional and non-conventional. Conventional energy resources include coal, oil, and natural gas, while non-conventional energy resources include solar, wind, and hydroelectric power.

concepts.title: Order of Energy Resources
concepts.description: To remember the order of energy resources, use the mnemonic 'COAL': Coal, Oil, Atomic, Lignite. This will help you recall the sequence in which these resources are generally used.

concepts.title: Difference between Minerals and Ores
concepts.description: Students often confuse minerals and ores. A mineral is a naturally occurring inorganic substance with a specific chemical composition and physical properties, while an ore is a mineral from which a metal can be extracted economically. For example, iron ore is a mineral from which iron can be extracted.

concepts.title: Energy Resources in India
concepts.description: India's primary energy resources include coal (54%), oil (28%), and natural gas (14%). Renewable energy sources, such as solar and wind power, account for a smaller percentage of the country's energy mix.

concepts.title: India's Energy Consumption Pattern
concepts.description: India's energy consumption pattern is dominated by coal, which accounts for over 50% of the country's total energy consumption. This is followed by oil and natural gas.

concepts.title: Importance of Energy Resources
concepts.description: Energy resources play a crucial role in the development of a country. They are essential for powering industries, transportation, and households, and are a key driver of economic growth.
